regime: Cool CPI Relief Rally / Broad Flip Long (Overnight Flush Reversed)
summary: Two tapes in one session. Overnight the weekend risk-off kept bleeding: the globex flush drove MNQ to a 29,303 low, MES to 7,531.75 and gold to 3,990.1, essentially tagging Monday's downside map targets before the cash open. Then June CPI printed cool at 8:30 ET (3.5% YoY vs 3.8% expected, core 2.6%, the biggest monthly drop since April 2020) and the tape flipped. MNQ rallied 1.24% to close 29,794.75 and flipped long above a 29,536 stop; MES added 0.49% and poked its 7,602 record with a 7,604 high before closing 7,590.5; gold bounced 1.24% to 4,058.5 but stalled under its 4,070 AVWAP; crude held its Hormuz bid, up 2.37% to tag the 80 handle. All four ATR stops are now long, but every flip is one session old and PPI lands tomorrow at 8:30 ET, so this is a bounce to respect, not chase.

keyLevels.label: MNQ ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED LONG; RTH close 29,795, +258 above. The line that now defines the bounce.

keyLevels.label: MNQ AVWAP / RTH High
keyLevels.note: Closed right at AVWAP after tagging a 29,896 high; the 30,000 round is the next magnet.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / Record
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED LONG; poked the 7,602 record (7,604 high), closed 7,590.5 just under it.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop (MGC1!)
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED LONG on the front contract but capped: closed 4,058.5, back under its 4,070 AVWAP.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Still LONG; close 79.86, tagged the 80.28 high and the 80 psych, ON high 81.28 above.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (long side)
setups.entry: Long with the flip on a pullback into the 29,536 (MNQ) / 7,544 (MES) stop, not a chase at the highs
setups.t1: MNQ 29,896 RTH high then 30,000; MES the 7,602 record then highs
setups.bias: BORDERLINE long. The flip is one session old and PPI is tomorrow; event risk caps size.

setups.name: AVWAP Bounce (MES / MNQ)
setups.entry: Long on a hold of the session AVWAP as support (MES 7,588, MNQ 29,798) with a reclaim bar
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. MES holds above its AVWAP; MNQ closed right at it. Confirm the hold before pressing into PPI.

setups.name: E3v2 Momentum Long (15min)
setups.entry: Only if the regime filter stays green and price holds above the flipped stops after the PPI reaction settles
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. Filter turned risk-on but the signal is event-contaminated into PPI.

event.name: CPI (June) released Tue Jul 14 08:30 ET; next scheduled: PPI (June) Wed Jul 15 08:30 ET
event.consensus: Headline +3.8% YoY expected; core +2.8% YoY expected
event.actual: Headline 3.5% YoY (from 4.2%), core 2.6% (from 2.9%), MoM -0.4%, the biggest monthly drop since April 2020; the energy component eased as US-Iran tensions cooled.
event.surprise: Cooler than expected on both headline and core, a clear downside inflation surprise that revived rate-cut hopes and risk appetite.
event.nqReaction: MNQ reversed an overnight flush (29,303 low) to close +1.24% at 29,794.75, flipping long above a 29,536 stop; tech led the bounce.
event.crossAsset: MES +0.49% poked its 7,602 record; MGC +1.24% but capped under its AVWAP; MCL +2.37% held its Hormuz bid to the 80 handle. Broad risk-on, though gold's soft follow-through is the tell.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: MES and MNQ both flipped long above their stops in the same session, real confirmation of the bounce rather than a single-index stop artifact.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap context, outside NAP stack)
observe.note: The weekend risk-off gap toward Friday's 30,069 is being worked back; the CPI bounce closed much of it. Context only, not a Net Alpha Pro signal.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252, outside NAP stack)
observe.note: The short-horizon trend flipped back up and the long-horizon base held; alignment is turning long again. Context only.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED. The book can lean with the flip but not chase it: every stop flipped long in one session and PPI is tomorrow at 8:30 ET. Add on pullbacks into the stops, not on the highs.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding; the book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact; disciplined pullback entries protect it into PPI.
risk.sizing: Any long is a pullback-into-the-stop entry at reduced size, never a chase at the 29,896 / 80.28 highs, and it should respect that the flip is one session old. Gold is the weakest long, capped under its 4,070 AVWAP, so it is the least sizeable of the four. Crude is long but headline-driven; keep it small.
risk.note: Week ahead: PPI (June) Wednesday July 15 at 8:30 ET is the next gate, one day after a cool CPI. Per prop rules avoid new risk in the news window and let the initial reaction settle. Trade the level reaction, not the headline.

psychology: The overnight flush was the trap. Anyone who leaned short into the globex lows (29,303 on MNQ, 3,990 on gold) got the downside targets they wanted, then watched a cool CPI rip it all back. That is the lesson of event days: the pre-print move is not the position, it is the setup for the repricing. Do not marry the overnight narrative, and do not chase the reversal candle at the highs either. The stops flipped long, but a one-session flip into another 8:30 print (PPI) tomorrow is a bounce to respect, not a trend to press. Wait for acceptance, a hold of the stop or AVWAP on a pullback, before you engage. Two clean closes beats one hopeful entry.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidenceNote
DirectionRISK-ON RECOVERY / FLIP LONGModerate-HighAll four flipped or held long after a cool CPI. MNQ +1.24%, MES +0.49%, MGC +1.24% (capped), MCL +2.37%. A one-session reversal of the weekend risk-off.
VolatilityELEVATED, NORMALIZINGModerateEvent-day ranges stayed wide. ATR(14): MNQ ~116, MES 16.2 to 18.8, MGC ~18, MCL 0.89 to 1.07. Contracting from the shock but not calm.
SessionPOST-EVENT (CPI)HighOvernight flush then a cool 8:30 ET CPI drove the reversal. Not a normal drift session.
InventorySHORTS COVERED / LONGS REBUILTModerate-HighThe globex flush cleared late shorts on the print; equity trend-followers flipped back long.
EventPOST_EVENT + SCHEDULEDHighJune CPI cool (3.5% YoY); PPI (June) Wednesday 08:30 ET is next.
LiquidityNORMALIZINGModeratePost-print volume high; spreads tightening from the shock, but another 8:30 print looms.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html:
LevelValue
Center (AVWAP / NYVWAP)29,798
Sigma42
+1s / +2s / +3s29,840 / 29,883 / 29,925
-1s / -2s / -3s29,756 / 29,714 / 29,671
SignalPrice closed 29,795, right on the center after tagging a 29,896 high (past the +2s). On a trend-up day, mean-reversion shorts at the +bands are counter-trend; a fade re-qualifies only on a stretched +3s (29,925) tag with an exhaustion bar and center reclaim, half size, not blind into PPI. Price-based (volume unavailable on the continuous contract).

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:
#Map row (targeted Tue Jul 14)ResultMeasured line
1MNQ stays below the 29,726 flipped-short stop and the 29,534 AVWAP on hourly closesTRIGGERED / MIXEDOvernight MNQ stayed below and flushed to a 29,303 low, tagging the 29,362 -2s and coming within 27 of the 29,276 -3s, then a cool CPI flipped it long. A 2-close back above 29,726 voided the short by the cash session.
2A soft CPI sparks a 2-close back above the 29,726 stop and the 29,620 +1sTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTCPI came in cool. MNQ 2-closed back above 29,726 and 29,620, and the relief bounce blew past the 29,752 retest to a 29,896 high, closing 29,794.75. Fell just short of the 30,000 round.
3MES holds below its 7,594 flipped-short stopTRIGGERED / MIXEDOvernight MES flushed to 7,531.75, tagging both the 7,544 -2s and the 7,530 -3s, then reversed to reclaim the 7,602 record intraday (7,604 high) before fading to close 7,590.5, just under the 7,594 line.
4MGC stays below its 4,049 stop and the 4,000 psychTRIGGERED / MIXEDOvernight gold slid to a 3,990.1 low, meeting the 3,992 -2s, then reversed hard and 2-closed back above 4,000 and the 4,049 stop to flip long and close 4,058.5. The downside target hit, then the stabilization tell fired.
5MCL holds above its 75.79 flipped-long stopTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTMCL held above 75.79 all session and ran to the 79.54 +2s and the 80.00 psych (80.28 high), closing 79.86. The Hormuz trend extended as mapped.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Graded the Jul 13 map (targeting Jul 14). Rows 2 and 5 TRIGGERED / PLAYED OUT (MNQ's soft-CPI bounce hit the 29,752 retest to a 29,896 high; MCL held 75.79 and ran to the 80 psych). Rows 1, 3 and 4 TRIGGERED / MIXED: the overnight globex first tagged the downside targets (MNQ 29,303 near the -3s, MES 7,531.75 at the -3s, MGC 3,990.1 at the -2s), then the cool CPI reversed all three and flipped them long. No rows failed outright.
